title: hieracium erythropodum subsp. coloratipes
intro: Hieracium erythropodum subsp. coloratipes, also known as a lesser hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. The leaves and flowers of this plant contain a range of medicinal compounds, including sesquiterpene lactones, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Hieracium erythropodum subsp. coloratipes include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions, promoting overall health and well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Hieracium erythropodum subsp. coloratipes are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
